identity.what_it_is: A structured AI development framework that provides persistent memory and deterministic workflows for software teams
identity.what_it_is_not: A code generation tool or a replacement for developer judgment and decision-making
core_jobs: Maintain project context across sessions so developers never re-explain what they are building
Orchestrate multi-step development workflows that produce auditable, consistent outputs
non_goals: Generating production-ready code without human review and approval gates
Replacing project management tools or serving as a team communication platform

invariants.description: Every workflow step must have an explicit human approval gate before destructive operations (commits, PRs, branch changes)
All AI-generated outputs must be traceable to the intent that produced them — no orphaned artifacts
Agent domain boundaries are enforced — no agent may perform work outside its declared responsibility

in_scope: User authentication and session management for web dashboard
Dashboard overview with real-time project metrics and status widgets
User settings management for preferences and notification configuration
out_of_scope: Mobile application — web-only for initial release
Third-party SSO integration — email/password only for MVP

deferred.capability: Team collaboration features (shared workspaces, role-based access)
deferred.rationale: Requires multi-tenancy architecture not included in MVP scope

deferred.capability: Webhook integrations with external project management tools
deferred.rationale: Deferred until API stability is confirmed post-launch

features.name: User Authentication
features.description: Complete authentication system allowing users to register, login, and recover access via email/password credentials with JWT session management

affected_workflows: All subsequent features depend on authenticated user context
blast_radius.notes: Must be implemented first — all other features gate on authenticated sessions

intent.p1: Users must be able to create accounts and authenticate securely so the system can maintain per-user context and project associations
intent.p2: Authentication failures must surface clear, actionable errors so users can self-recover without support intervention
intent.p3: Session tokens must expire and rotate to prevent unauthorized access from stale credentials

must_not_break: Existing database schema if any migrations run

success_scenarios.given: A new user visits the registration page
success_scenarios.when: They submit valid email and password
success_scenarios.then: Account is created and user receives a JWT token to access the dashboard

success_scenarios.given: A registered user attempts login with correct credentials
success_scenarios.then: Server returns a valid JWT and user is redirected to the dashboard

success_scenarios.given: A user has forgotten their password
success_scenarios.when: They submit their email to the reset flow
success_scenarios.then: They receive a reset link and can set a new password within 1 hour

failure_conditions: Registration with duplicate email returns a 409 conflict — not a 500 server error
Login with invalid credentials returns 401 — not a generic error that leaks account existence

behaviors.description: Rate limit login attempts to prevent brute force
behaviors.interaction: User submits more than 5 failed login attempts within 10 minutes
behaviors.observable_outcome: Account is temporarily locked and user receives an email notification

behaviors.interaction: Client detects token expiry approaching within 5 minutes
behaviors.observable_outcome: Client silently refreshes token without requiring re-login

features.name: Dashboard Overview
features.description: Primary landing page displaying real-time project metrics, workflow status, and activity feed in a configurable widget grid layout

existing_systems: Requires F1 authentication to be in place
affected_workflows: First screen seen after login — directly impacts onboarding experience
blast_radius.notes: Widget layout preferences stored per-user — requires user settings table

intent.p1: Authenticated users must see an immediate overview of their project health so they can orient quickly at the start of each session
intent.p2: Dashboard data must reflect the current state without requiring a manual refresh so users trust the information
intent.p3: Widget layout must be persistent across sessions so users do not re-configure their view each time

in_scope: Metrics widgets for feature count, open issues, and recent activity
Configurable widget grid with drag-and-drop reordering
Real-time updates via WebSocket for active sessions
out_of_scope: Custom widget development by end users
must_not_break: API response time SLA of <200ms for dashboard load

success_scenarios.given: An authenticated user navigates to the dashboard
success_scenarios.then: All widgets display current data within 2 seconds with no loading errors

success_scenarios.given: A user has configured a custom widget layout
success_scenarios.when: They log out and log back in
success_scenarios.then: Their configured layout is restored exactly as they left it

failure_conditions: If the stats API is slow, widgets degrade gracefully with skeleton loaders — the page never shows a blank screen
WebSocket disconnect shows a reconnecting indicator — not a stale data warning that alarms the user

behaviors.description: Widget data refresh on tab focus
behaviors.interaction: User switches back to the dashboard tab after being away for more than 5 minutes
behaviors.observable_outcome: All widgets silently refresh their data without a full page reload

behaviors.interaction: A newly registered user views the dashboard before creating any projects
behaviors.observable_outcome: Dashboard shows an onboarding prompt rather than empty widgets

features.name: Settings Management
features.description: User preference management system allowing profile editing, notification configuration, and account management actions

existing_systems: Reads and writes to user record established by F1
affected_workflows: Notification preferences affect email delivery for password reset and alerts
blast_radius.notes: Non-blocking — can ship after F1 and F2 without impacting core workflows

intent.p1: Users must be able to update their profile information so the system reflects accurate identity data across all artifacts
intent.p2: Notification preferences must be respected immediately so users are not spammed after opting out
intent.p3: Account deletion must be available so users can exercise data control in compliance with privacy requirements

in_scope: Profile editing (display name, email, avatar URL)
must_not_break: Email used for notifications must remain consistent with authentication email unless explicitly changed

success_scenarios.given: An authenticated user opens the settings page
success_scenarios.when: They update their display name and save
success_scenarios.then: The new name appears immediately in the navigation and all subsequent views

success_scenarios.when: A system event that would trigger a notification occurs
success_scenarios.then: No email is sent to the user

failure_conditions: Changing email requires re-verification — the old email remains active until the new one is confirmed
Account deletion is a two-step confirmation — not a single click action

behaviors.interaction: User edits settings but navigates away without saving
behaviors.observable_outcome: Browser confirms navigation with a warning that changes will be lost
Preference is persisted and applied to the next eligible event without requiring page reload
